---
title: "Mouse hindcast"
subtitle: Autumn 2025
description: "Regional seasonal hindcasts of house mouse abundance in the Australian grainbelt"
#date: last-modified
title-block-banner: "white"
#title-block-banner-color: "black" # title font colour
format: 
  html:
    self-contained: true
    theme: cosmo
    fontcolor: black
    fig-width: 8  
    fig-height: 20
css: styles.css
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: source
toc: TRUE
---
```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
#| include: FALSE

## load packages
library(targets)
library(dplyr)
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
library(flextable)
library(scico)
library(viridis)

# set the default theme for ggplot objects 
theme_update(panel.grid = element_blank(), 
            legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
#| label: load
#| message: false
#| include: FALSE

## load targets 
targets::tar_load(
  c(model_data,
    model_predictions_trend,
    model_predictions_region, 
    model_predictions_ae_zone))

# read in australian outline shapefile and adjust depending on how data has been filtered 
aus_shp = sf::read_sf("raw_data/predictor_variables/australian_borders/aus_outline_states.shp") |> 
    sf::st_transform(crs = "EPSG:4326") |> 
    dplyr::filter(ST_NAME != "WA")
  
# town / city centres for labelling
town_labels = sf::st_as_sf(read.csv("raw_data/town_labels.csv"), coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326", remove = FALSE)
```


```{r}
#| label: site_data

site_data_for_plot <- model_data |>
  
  # remove unsurveyed seasons for each site
  filter(!(is.na(mice_prop))) |>
  
  # take relevant columns 
  dplyr::select(longitude, latitude, season, season_year_adj, mice_prop) |>
  
  # new column for whether any mice signs were detected
  group_by(longitude, latitude, season_year_adj) %>%
  mutate(mice01 = if_else(sum(mice_prop) > (0.001 * n()), "yes", "no")) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  
  # just one row per site, subsite, season_year_adj
  unique() %>%
  
  # make spatial for plotting
  sf::st_as_sf(., coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = "EPSG:4326") %>%
  
  # make factor
  dplyr::mutate(season_year_adj = factor(season_year_adj, levels = levels(model_predictions_ae_zone$season_year_adj))) 
  

```




```{r}
#| label: fig_3cat
#| layout-ncol: 1
#| fig-cap: 
#|   - "Predicted relative abundance of house mice in the current and future season, across modified GRDC agroecological zones. Black dots represents sites monitored during that season."

model_predictions_ae_zone |>
  
  ggplot() + 
  
  # first plot Australian states as outline in the background
  geom_sf(data = aus_shp, fill = "white") + 
  
  # remove axis labels (long, lat)
  labs(y = "", x = "") +
  
  # plot predictions within each ae zone
  geom_sf(aes(fill = factor(cat3, levels = c("Low", "Moderate", "High"), ordered = TRUE)), show.legend = TRUE) +
  
  # new colour scale for predictions
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Low" = "#91cf60", "Moderate" = "#ffeda0", "High" = "#f03b20"), 
                      breaks = c("Low", "Moderate", "High"),
                      name = "Relative abundance",
                      drop = FALSE) + # don't drop missing levels - always have low med and high
  
  # add site data (after filter to match times in prediction data)
  geom_sf(data = filter(site_data_for_plot, season_year_adj %in% unique(model_predictions_ae_zone$season_year_adj)), shape = 16, size = 0.7) +

  # crop map 
  coord_sf(xlim = c(128, 155), ylim = c(-40, -24), expand = FALSE) +
  
  # if multiple plots
  facet_wrap(~season_year_adj, ncol = 4)

```



### Predicted current situation

```{r}
#| label: plot_mean_predictions
#| fig-cap: 
#|   - "Predicted relative abundance of house mice in the current and future season, across modified GRDC agroecological zones. Grey dots represents sites monitored during that season."
  
  model_predictions_ae_zone |>
    
  ggplot() + 
  
  # first plot Australian states as outline in the background
  geom_sf(data = aus_shp, fill = "white") + 
  
  # remove axis labels (long, lat)
  labs(y = "", x = "") +
  
  # plot predictions within each ae zone
  geom_sf(aes(fill = log(pred_mean))) +
  
  # new colour scale for predictions (NOTE GETTING SCALE FROM PREDS FOR ALL TIMES!)
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    limits=c(log(min(model_predictions_ae_zone$pred_mean)), log(max(model_predictions_ae_zone$pred_mean))),
    breaks=c(log(min(model_predictions_ae_zone$pred_mean)), log(max(model_predictions_ae_zone$pred_mean))), 
    labels=c("Low", "High"), name = "Relative abundance\n(log-scaled)") +
  
  # add site data (after filter to match times in prediction data)
  geom_sf(data = filter(site_data_for_plot, season_year_adj %in% unique(model_predictions_ae_zone$season_year_adj)), shape = 16, size = 0.7, col = "grey75") +
  
  # crop map 
  coord_sf(xlim = c(128, 155), ylim = c(-40, -24), expand = FALSE) +
  
  facet_wrap(~season_year_adj, ncol = 4)
```




### Probability of mouse plague
```{r}
#| label: plot_prob_threshold
#| fig-cap:
#|   - "Predicted probability of mouse abundance being in a high state, across modified GRDC agroecological zones. Grey dots represents sites monitored during that season."

  model_predictions_ae_zone %>%

  ggplot() +

  # first plot Australian states as outline in the background
  geom_sf(data = aus_shp, fill = "white") +

  # remove axis labels (long, lat)
  labs(y = "", x = "") +

  # plot predictions within each ae zone
  geom_sf(aes(fill = pr_threshold * 100)) + # change to percentage

  # new colour scale for predictions
  scale_fill_scico(
    palette = "vik",
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100),
    name = "Outbreak probability (%)") +

  # add site data (after filter to match times in prediction data)
  geom_sf(data = filter(site_data_for_plot, season_year_adj %in% unique(model_predictions_ae_zone$season_year_adj)), shape = 16, size = 0.7, col = "grey75") +

  # crop map
  coord_sf(xlim = c(128, 155), ylim = c(-40, -24), expand = FALSE) +

  # facet by season
  facet_wrap(~season_year_adj, ncol = 4)

```


### Predicted time series by agroecological zone

```{r}
#| label: plot_timeseries_aez
#| fig-height: 14
#| fig-cap:
#|   - "Mean prediction (line) and 95% credible interval (ribbon) of relative mouse abundance for each agroecological zone over time. Points show the mean observed proportion of detections per ae_zone and season."

# use the factor levels from the full prediction object so the x-axis is
# ordered chronologically and both datasets share the same scale.
# ae_zone predictions span the full training period so their levels are the
# most complete — used as the reference in all three time-series plots below
sy_levels <- levels(model_predictions_ae_zone$season_year_adj)

# aggregate observed detections to ae_zone × season level, applying the
# same factor levels as the predictions so geom_point positions correctly
obs_aez <- model_data |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(mice_prop)) |>
  dplyr::group_by(ae_zone, season_year_adj) |>
  dplyr::summarise(obs_mean = mean(mice_prop, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(season_year_adj = factor(season_year_adj, levels = sy_levels, ordered = TRUE))

# drop sf geometry — not needed for a line plot
model_predictions_ae_zone |>
  sf::st_drop_geometry() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = season_year_adj, group = ae_zone)) +

  # 95% credible interval ribbon
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = pred_lower, ymax = pred_upper),
              fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +

  # mean prediction line
  geom_line(aes(y = pred_mean), colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.7) +

  # observed mean detection rate per ae_zone-season
  geom_point(data = obs_aez,
             aes(x = season_year_adj, y = obs_mean),
             colour = "black", size = 1.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +

  labs(y = "Relative abundance", x = "") +

  # rotate season labels to avoid overlap on a long time axis
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 6)) +

  # one panel per ae_zone, 2 columns
  facet_wrap(~ ae_zone, ncol = 2)
```


### Predicted time series by region

```{r}
#| label: plot_timeseries_region
#| fig-height: 18
#| fig-cap:
#|   - "Mean prediction (line) and 95% credible interval (ribbon) of relative mouse abundance for each region over time. Points show the mean observed proportion of detections per region and season."

# aggregate observed detections to region × season, aligned to reference levels
obs_region <- model_data |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(mice_prop)) |>
  dplyr::group_by(region, ae_zone, season_year_adj) |>
  dplyr::summarise(obs_mean = mean(mice_prop, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(season_year_adj = factor(season_year_adj, levels = sy_levels, ordered = TRUE))

model_predictions_region |>
  sf::st_drop_geometry() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = season_year_adj, group = region)) +

  # 95% credible interval ribbon
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = pred_lower, ymax = pred_upper),
              fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.25) +

  # mean prediction line
  geom_line(aes(y = pred_mean), colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.7) +

  # observed mean detection rate per region-season
  geom_point(data = obs_region,
             aes(x = season_year_adj, y = obs_mean),
             colour = "black", size = 1.0, inherit.aes = FALSE) +

  labs(y = "Relative abundance", x = "") +

  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 5)) +

  # one panel per region, 3 columns
  facet_wrap(~ region, ncol = 3)
```


### Predicted time series by site

```{r}
#| label: plot_timeseries_site
#| fig-height: 18
#| fig-cap:
#|   - "Mean prediction (line) and 95% credible interval (ribbon) of relative mouse abundance for each site over time, grouped within region. Points show the mean observed proportion of detections per site and season."

# aggregate observed detections to site × season (multiple survey nights per
# site-season are averaged), aligned to reference factor levels
obs_site <- model_data |>
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(mice_prop)) |>
  dplyr::group_by(region, site, subsite, season_year_adj) |>
  dplyr::summarise(obs_mean = mean(mice_prop, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  dplyr::mutate(season_year_adj = factor(season_year_adj, levels = sy_levels, ordered = TRUE))

model_predictions_trend |>
  sf::st_drop_geometry() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = season_year_adj, group = trend)) +

  # 95% credible interval ribbon — use high transparency because many sites overlap
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = pred_lower, ymax = pred_upper),
              fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.08) +

  # mean prediction line per site
  geom_line(aes(y = pred_mean), colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.7) +

  # observed mean detection rate per site-season
  geom_point(data = obs_site,
             aes(x = season_year_adj, y = obs_mean, group = paste(site, subsite)),
             colour = "black", size = 0.6, alpha = 0.5, inherit.aes = FALSE) +

  labs(y = "Relative abundance", x = "") +

  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 5)) +

  # facet by region so sites within the same region are shown together
  facet_wrap(~ region, ncol = 3)
```
